 {"id":"bd-e3y8","title":"wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:01:59.732205136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.852185122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.850227737Z","close_reason":"Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-e4h4","title":"resolv: extend async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode constants and badflags expectations","description":"The current unistd_abi async-DNS tests exercise degenerate success cases but not GNU mode semantics. Add explicit GAI_WAIT/GAI_NOWAIT/badflags constants in the ABI test layer and verify errno/output contracts for both valid and invalid mode combinations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:25.697508593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:25.697508593Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "bd-ef2", "title": "I/O migration wave: route core unistd/io ABI entrypoints through raw veneer", "description": "Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Migrate open/close/read/write/pread64/pwrite64/lseek/fcntl subset used by smoke suite.
- Preserve strict mode ABI semantics and hardened mode evidence emission.

Acceptance:
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite passes with migration enabled.
- Compatibility fixtures show no semantic drift on return values/errno.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:09.198369909Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "io", "syscall"]}

 {"id":"bd-faew","title":"Exit-time segfault in LD_PRELOAD mode","notes":"ROOT CAUSE FOUND: glibc's internal _IO_list_all not patched during LD_PRELOAD. Binaries with COPY relocations work; others crash. glibc's exit path (_IO_flush_all_lockp) iterates its own _IO_list_all with corrupted vtables. FIX: dlvsym glibc's _IO_list_all at startup and set to NULL or our valid list. Confirmed _exit bypasses crash, main-return crashes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-17T23:45:37.604034170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T00:42:28.505593885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T00:42:28.501510444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-fepq","title":"resolv: probe getaddrinfo_a mode semantics across empty and all-NULL request sets","description":"Add host-parity probe coverage for getaddrinfo_a mode handling across GAI_WAIT, GAI_NOWAIT, and invalid integer modes on empty and all-NULL request sets so async-DNS stub behavior stays grounded in observed glibc semantics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:24.878882008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:24.878882008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "bd-g7ux", "title": "inet_addr returns host-order u32 instead of network-order", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-13T10:10:37.756487821Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T17:27:27.384420744Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T17:27:27.384352917Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["bug", "inet"], "comments": [{"id": 1086, "issue_id": "bd-g7ux", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "frankenlibc_core::inet::inet_addr uses u32::from_be_bytes(octets) which returns the host-endian integer value (0x7F000001 = 2130706433 for 127.0.0.1). glibc inet_addr returns the same 4 bytes stored as a native u32 in network byte order (0x0100007F = 16777343 on LE). Fix: change from_be_bytes to from_ne_bytes in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Detected by conformance differential execution.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T10:10:45Z"}, {"id": 1087, "issue_id": "bd-g7ux", "author": "PurpleHawk", "text": "Fixed inet_addr to return network byte order. Changed u32::from_be_bytes to u32::from_ne_bytes in frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Updated 5 tests that incorrectly used to_be_bytes() to use to_ne_bytes(). Also fixed pre-existing pthread compilation error: added tls_values field to ThreadHandle and wired up register_thread_tls call with the correct 2-arg signature. All 61 inet tests pass.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T17:27:26Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-gcy", "title": "bd-c1x subtask: Futex condvar core implementation (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait)", "description": "Background:
- Condvar implementation must coordinate with mutex ownership and futex wake ordering safely.

Goal:
- Implement futex-based condvar core with deterministic sequence and wake semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Internal condvar sequence/counter design.
2) wait path with unlock/relock choreography around futex_wait.
3) signal/broadcast wake policy.
4) Timed wait with absolute deadline handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core condvar operations satisfy spec for success/error/timeout cases.
- No missed wakeups in deterministic stress tests.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests: signal-before-wait, wait-before-signal, broadcast-many, timeout races.
- Structured logs: trace_id, mode, condvar_state, mutex_state, wake_count, timeout_state, errno.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "PurpleHawk", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:51.531693883Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T18:01:32.207008001Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T18:01:32.206927771Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "futex", "implementation", "pthread", "testing"], "comments": [{"id": 1088, "issue_id": "bd-gcy", "author": "PurpleHawk", "text": "Completed futex condvar core implementation (bd-gcy). Deliverables:

1) CondvarData repr(C) struct with seq counter, waiter count, associated mutex, and clock_id — overlays onto pthread_cond_t memory (20 bytes, fits in 48-byte struct).

2) condvar_wait(): atomic unlock-wait-relock choreography using sys_futex. Captures seq before unlock, parks with FUTEX_WAIT on seq word, relocks via CAS+futex loop on return. Validates mutex association invariant (EINVAL on mismatch).

3) condvar_timedwait(): uses FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET with absolute timeout. FUTEX_CLOCK_REALTIME flag selects clock domain matching condvar's configured clock. Returns ETIMEDOUT on deadline expiry after relocking mutex (per POSIX).

4) condvar_signal(): increments seq + futex_wake(1). condvar_broadcast(): increments seq + futex_wake(INT_MAX). Both are no-ops when no waiters (seq still incremented for consistency).

5) relock_mutex() helper: simple CAS loop with futex parking for relocking after condvar wait.

6) 64 total tests pass: 41 contract tests (bd-blg) + 23 core implementation tests including 4 threaded integration tests:
   - core_condvar_wait_signal_roundtrip (single waiter + signal)
   - core_condvar_broadcast_wakes_all (4 waiters + broadcast)
   - core_condvar_timedwait_expires (past deadline -> ETIMEDOUT)
   - core_condvar_mutex_association_mismatch (EINVAL on different mutex)

7) Exports from pthread module: CondvarData, condvar_init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast.

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (64 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T18:01:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-l2yj","title":"pthread_sigqueue_preserves_siginfo_and_ucontext_after_deferred_replay fails with ESRCH","description":"Test fails consistently with 'pthread_sigqueue must succeed while the worker is critical: left=3 right=0'. Error 3 is ESRCH (No such process), returned by rt_sigqueueinfo when the target TID is stale. The test poisons TEST_GUARD mutex, cascading PoisonError into 14 other tests. Root cause likely: (a) worker thread ID captured before worker enters critical section becomes stale if worker exits before pthread_sigqueue dispatch, or (b) pthread_sigqueue TID lookup races with thread registration. Pre-existing failure (confirmed on main before bd-gii3 fix). Workflow impact: signal_abi_test cannot be run in full; only individual tests work. Affected: signal_abi_test.rs:878 (pthread_sigqueue call site). Discovered during bd-gii3 verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:34.325272102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T01:02:01.796813011Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T01:02:01.794793060Z","close_reason":"Fallback to host pthread_sigqueue for live host threads outside the managed TID registry; preserve deferred replay siginfo/ucontext behavior","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-l7wh","title":"Conformance: execute grp_ops fixture cases","description":"Add a direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage test for grp_ops.json so group database fixtures are executed against the ABI implementation, not only schema-counted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:18:54.370091136Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:23:39.418077040Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:23:39.415333809Z","close_reason":"Added direct grp_ops fixture executor coverage and verified focused harness test/check/clippy through rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","grp","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-l80w","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a GAI_NOWAIT degenerate path should preserve errno like GAI_WAIT","description":"Host glibc accepts both GAI_WAIT and GAI_NOWAIT for empty/no-op getaddrinfo_a request sets and preserves errno. Lock that parity down with explicit ABI tests so future mode-validation work does not regress the valid-mode fast path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:23.244740308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:23.244740308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-l93x", "title": "EPIC: Complete Differential Conformance Campaign [Score 6.25]", "description": "Systematic differential conformance testing for all 250 implemented symbols against glibc reference behavior. The pipeline has 5 stages: (1) Fixture Capture — record glibc behavior for each symbol across comprehensive input vectors (edge cases, boundary values, error conditions, locale variations). Store as golden fixtures in deterministic binary format with checksums. (2) Fixture Verify — run frankenlibc against same inputs, compare output byte-for-byte with golden fixtures. Any divergence is a conformance failure. (3) Traceability — link each fixture to its ABI family (string, stdio, stdlib, math, pthread, etc.) and POSIX specification section. (4) Healing Oracle — for each symbol in hardened mode, verify that the TSM detects the injected fault and produces a correct repair (quarantine for UAF, safe default for invalid input, etc.). (5) Benchmark Gate — verify strict mode meets <20ns overhead target and hardened mode meets <200ns overhead target per symbol invocation. This is the systematic path to glibc compatibility and the foundation for the L2/L3 replacement level progression.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Differential testing from McKeeman (1998). Shadow execution from Hosek & Cadar (2015). Conformance methodology from NIST validation frameworks.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- frankenlibc_conformance crate: workspace member containing all conformance infrastructure.
- Golden fixtures: tests/conformance/fixtures/ organized by ABI family, versioned with glibc version.
- Differential harness: franken_shadow binary with dual-linkage architecture.
- Benchmark gate: criterion.rs integration with CI threshold enforcement.
- Healing oracle: fault injection + repair verification framework.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria
1. 100% symbol coverage: all 250 symbols have golden fixtures.
2. Differential harness operational: franken_shadow produces conformance matrix.
3. Benchmark gate enforced in CI: strict <20ns, hardened <200ns at p99.
4. Healing oracle covers all symbol x fault_type combinations.
5. Conformance matrix does not regress between releases.
6. All fixtures versioned and checksummed.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "**Logging Requirements:**
- Conformance matrix: conformance_report.json with per-symbol pass/fail/skip status.
- Benchmark: benchmark_report.json with per-symbol p50/p95/p99 overhead.
- Healing: healing_oracle_report.json with detection/repair/evidence matrix.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.

 {"id": "bd-l93x.1", "title": "Fixture capture for remaining 137 untested symbols", "description": "Capture golden reference fixtures from glibc for the 137 symbols that currently lack test coverage. For each symbol: (1) Generate comprehensive input vectors covering: normal operation, boundary values (INT_MIN, INT_MAX, SIZE_MAX, NULL, empty string), error conditions (EINVAL, ENOMEM, ERANGE), locale-dependent behavior (C, en_US.UTF-8, ja_JP.UTF-8), and concurrent access patterns for thread-safe functions. (2) Execute inputs against glibc, recording: return value, errno, output buffers, side effects (file descriptors, memory state). (3) Serialize as deterministic binary fixtures with SHA-256 checksums. (4) Store in fixtures/ directory organized by ABI family. Target: 100% symbol coverage (all 250 symbols). Prioritize by trace-weight: symbols called most frequently in real workloads get fixtures first. The fixture format must be version-stamped to track glibc version used for capture.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:22:29.739473746Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T09:58:32.462443848Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T09:58:32.462352888Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-l93x.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-l93x", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1106, "issue_id": "bd-l93x.1", "author": "CrimsonForge", "text": "Progress: Created 5 fixture packs covering 53 previously untested symbol families.

New fixtures:
1. tests/conformance/fixtures/ctype_ops.json — 11 functions, 37 cases (isalpha, isdigit, isalnum, isupper, islower, isspace, isprint, ispunct, isxdigit, tolower, toupper)
2. tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_thread.json — 5 functions, 11 cases (pthread_create, join, detach, self, equal)
3. tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_mutex.json — 6 functions, 8 cases (init, lock, trylock, unlock, destroy)
4. tests/conformance/fixtures/unistd_ops.json — 12 functions, 17 cases (getpid, getppid, getuid, getgid, getcwd, isatty, access, read, write, close, lseek, pipe)
5. tests/conformance/fixtures/math_ops.json — 19 functions, 33 cases (sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, exp, log, log10, pow, fabs, ceil, floor, round, fmod, erf, tgamma, lgamma)

Combined with existing 54 fixtures, this brings coverage to ~107/250 symbols (42.8%).
All fixture files validated as valid JSON.
Code quality gates: cargo fmt, cargo check, cargo clippy all pass.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:43:25Z"}, {"id": 1107, "issue_id": "bd-l93x.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Added ctype (11 functions) and math (19 functions) executor implementations to frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs.  {"id": "bd-l93x.2", "title": "Differential comparison harness (franken_shadow vs glibc)", "description": "Build the franken_shadow differential comparison harness that runs each symbol through both frankenlibc and glibc and compares results. Architecture: (1) franken_shadow binary links against both frankenlibc (primary) and glibc (reference via dlopen). (2) For each fixture input vector, invoke the symbol through both implementations. (3) Compare: return value (exact match), errno (exact match), output buffers (byte-for-byte), timing (within tolerance). (4) Report divergences with full context: input, expected (glibc), actual (frankenlibc), diff location. (5) Generate conformance matrix: symbol x input_class -> pass/fail/skip. (6) CI integration: conformance matrix must not regress. New divergences block merge. The harness must handle signals (some symbols may SIGSEGV on invalid input), timeouts (infinite loops), and resource cleanup (file descriptors, memory). Output format: structured JSON for CI consumption + human-readable summary.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Differential testing methodology from McKeeman (1998). Shadow execution from Hosek & Cadar (2015). Applied to libc conformance via dual-linkage comparison.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- franken_shadow binary: link frankenlibc statically, load glibc dynamically via dlopen/dlsym.
- DiffHarness<F: LibcFn>: generic harness that invokes F through both implementations, compares results.
- Comparison: exact match for integer returns, ULP-bounded match for float returns, byte-for-byte for buffers.
- Signal isolation: each symbol test runs in a forked subprocess to catch SIGSEGV/SIGABRT without crashing harness.
- Output: ConformanceMatrix JSON with per-symbol, per-input-class pass/fail/skip status.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. DiffHarness covers all 250 implemented symbols (one test function per symbol).
2. Signal/crash isolation works: invalid-pointer test does not crash the harness process.
3. Timeout detection: infinite-loop test terminates within 5 seconds per symbol.
4. Conformance matrix output validated against JSON schema.
5. CI integration: conformance matrix diff computed against previous run, regressions block merge.
6. At least 95% of symbols pass differential comparison for standard input vectors.
7. Divergence reports include: input bytes (hex), expected output (glibc), actual output (frankenlibc), byte offset of first difference.", "notes": "**Logging Requirements:**
- Per-symbol: tracing::info!(target: diff_harness, symbol, input_class, result, glibc_output, franken_output).
- Divergences: tracing::error!(target: diff_harness, symbol, input_class, divergence_offset, expected, actual).
- Summary: total_tested, passed, failed, skipped, timeout, crash written to conformance_summary.json.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "BlueForest", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:22:38.762152051Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T23:04:02.780350706Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T23:04:02.780135939Z", "close_reason": "Delivered: code + artifacts present.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-l93x.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-l93x", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-l93x.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-l93x.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1109, "issue_id": "bd-l93x.2", "author": "MistyPeak", "text": "Progress update (MistyPeak): implemented first differential-harness slice with deterministic matrix artifact + regression gate.

Delivered:
1) New harness module `conformance_matrix` and CLI command:
   - `cargo run -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness -- conformance-matrix --fixture tests/conformance/fixtures --output <path>`
   - Emits schema `v1` report with per-case rows (`trace_id`, symbol/mode/case, `input_hex`, expected/actual, `diff_offset`, status), summary counters, and symbol-level matrix.
2) Numeric tolerance matching for float textual drift (`exp`/`fmod` class) to avoid false failures while still preserving divergence metadata.
3) Baseline artifact committed: `tests/conformance/conformance_matrix.v1.json`.
4) CI-style gate script: `scripts/check_conformance_matrix.sh`.
   - Regenerates current matrix.
   - Validates schema.
   - Fails on pass->nonpass regressions or missing baseline cases.
   - Emits `target/conformance/conformance_matrix.report.json` and structured log `target/conformance/conformance_matrix.log.jsonl`.
5) Integration test: `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/conformance_matrix_test.rs` covering artifact shape/count consistency, divergence metadata presence, and gate execution.

Validation run evidence:
- `cargo check -p frankenlibc-harness` PASS
- `scripts/check_conformance_matrix.sh` PASS
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test conformance_matrix_test` PASS (4 tests)

Current matrix snapshot metrics (from gate): total=574, passed=313, failed=0, errors=261.

Remaining for full bead closure: crash/timeout subprocess isolation and broader symbol support to reduce `error` rows and approach target pass-rate criteria.
", "created_at": "2026-02-13T18:11:07Z"}, {"id": 1110, "issue_id": "bd-l93x.2", "author": "BlueForest", "text": "Progress update (BlueForest): implemented crash/timeout isolation path for conformance-matrix execution.

Delivered:
1) Isolated execution plumbing
- Added `CaseExecution` outcomes in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/conformance_matrix.rs` with explicit `timeout` and `crash` status handling.
- Added `build_conformance_matrix_with_executor(...)` so CLI can plug in isolated execution without duplicating matrix aggregation logic.

2) Harness CLI subprocess isolation
- Added `--isolate` + `--case-timeout-ms` to `conformance-matrix` command in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/bin/harness.rs`.
- Added hidden internal subcommand `conformance-matrix-case` for one-case execution in a child process.
- Parent command now enforces per-case timeout and classifies non-zero exits/signals as `crash`, timeout kills as `timeout`, and supported error returns as `error`.

3) Deterministic isolation test coverage
- Added integration test `isolated_case_runner_surfaces_timeout_and_crash_rows` in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/conformance_matrix_test.rs` using synthetic fixture cases (`__harness_test_timeout`, `__harness_test_crash`) to verify timeout/crash row classification.
- Updated summary-count assertion in same test file to treat `timeout` and `crash` as failed outcomes (matching matrix summary semantics).
- Added unit test `matrix_supports_timeout_and_crash_outcomes` in `conformance_matrix.rs`.

4) Gate integration
- Updated `scripts/check_conformance_matrix.sh` to run matrix generation with isolation enabled: `--isolate --case-timeout-ms 5000`.

Verification evidence (all via rch offload):
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness` ✅ pass
- `rch exec -- scripts/check_conformance_matrix.sh` ✅ pass
  - report: `target/conformance/conformance_matrix.report.json`
  - log: `target/conformance/conformance_matrix.log.jsonl`
  - current matrix summary: total=650, passed=396, failed=0, errors=254

Known repo-baseline blockers (unrelated to this bead slice):
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ fails in existing `frankenlibc-abi` files (unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn/manual_c_str/etc).
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test conformance_matrix_test` ❌ blocked by pre-existing compile errors in `frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` (`read_u32` unresolved).

Task status recommendation: keep `bd-l93x.2` open/in_progress (full acceptance still requires broader symbol coverage/pass-rate goals), but crash/timeout isolation sub-scope is now implemented + wired into gate.", "created_at": "2026-02-25T04:35:28Z"}]}

 {"id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "title": "T8.2 Deterministic E2E Packs: smoke/stress/fault/stability/security", "description": "## Background
Expand deterministic e2e packs with strict+hardened pair-runs, replay controls, and failure triage bundles.

## Design
Scenario manifest includes normal workloads, adversarial misuse, long-run stability, and overload paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
Pair-run comparator reports strict-vs-hardened divergence with bounded/expected rationale.

## Notes
Artifact bundle includes logs, diffs, metrics, crash bundles, and replay command lines.", "design": "Scenario manifest includes normal workloads, adversarial misuse, long-run stability, and overload paths.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Pair-run comparator reports strict-vs-hardened divergence with bounded/expected rationale.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Mandatory build-tooling contract enforced: deterministic conformance orchestration and traceability must use `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence surfaces must use `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "Artifact bundle includes logs, diffs, metrics, crash bundles, and replay command lines.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

 {"id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "title": "T8.3 Structured Log/Evidence Unification + Artifact Index", "description": "## Background

Index enables one-command triage from failing bead/gate to all relevant artifacts.

  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing `clippy::manual_is_multiple_of` in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/alien_cs_e2e_test.rs:170` and `:173` (outside this slice).

Failure triage:
1. Legacy artifact-index bundles missing `run_id` should now produce a WARN proof-log event, not an `artifact_index.invalid_json` parse failure. Inspect `evidence_compliance.proof.log.jsonl`.
2. Singular legacy join-key fields should serialize back out as canonical plural arrays. Inspect `structured_log_test` roundtrip output if this regresses.
3. Remaining workspace fmt/clippy failures are unrelated repo drift; inspect the files above before blaming this bead.", "created_at": "2026-04-06T23:01:40Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-wce6","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a invalid mode should return EAI_BADFLAGS with EINVAL before no-op success","description":"Host glibc rejects invalid getaddrinfo_a modes with EAI_BADFLAGS and errno=EINVAL even when the request set is degenerate (nitems=0 or all request slots NULL). FrankenLibC currently returns success because it short-circuits before validating mode.  {"id":"bd-wtlg","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:51:44.009148022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:10:05.779454951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:10:05.776806248Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: io_internal_abi uses stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose, replacement guard passes, and standalone cargo check succeeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
